To make the requested changes, we need to modify the footer section of the document. 

Original XML/Text:
```xml
[FOOTER] Gujarat Technological University                   
```

New XML/Text:
```xml
<w:p>
  <w:pPr>
    <w:sectPr>
      <w:footerReference w:type="default" />
      <w:pgNumType w:fmt="lowerRoman" w:start="1" />
    </w:sectPr>
  </w:pPr>
  <w:r>
    <w:t xml:space="preserve">Gujarat Technological University</w:t>
  </w:r>
  <w:r>
    <w:tab />
    <w:t xml:space="preserve">Page </w:t>
    <w:fldSimple w:instr="PAGE \* Roman ">
      <w:r>
        <w:t xml:space="preserve">i</w:t>
      </w:r>
    </w:fldSimple>
  </w:r>
  <w:r>
    <w:tab />
    <w:t xml:space="preserve">SAL College of Engineering</w:t>
  </w:r>
</w:p>
```

From the DECLARATION to TABLE OF CONTENTS, the footer will have the Gujarat Technological University on the left side, the page number in lower Roman numerals in the center, and SAL College of Engineering on the right side.

For the page numbering, we've added the `<w:pgNumType>` tag to control the format and starting number of the page numbers.

Note: The actual implementation may vary depending on the specific requirements and the version of the document processing software being used. The provided code is in OOXML format, which is commonly used in Microsoft Word documents.

To achieve the desired changes, you need to modify the XML code in the document.xml file. 

For the section from "DECLARATION" to "TABLE OF CONTENTS", you want to add Roman page numbers and modify the footer to include "Gujarat Technological University" on the left side and "SAL College of Engineering" on the right side.

Original XML:
```xml
<w:footer>
    <w:p>
        <w:r>
            <w:t>Gujarat Technological University</w:t>
        </w:r>
    </w:p>
</w:footer>
```

New XML:
```xml
<w:footer>
    <w:p>
        <w:pPr>
            <w:sectPr>
                <w:pgNumType w:fmt='lowerRoman' w:start='1'/>
            </w:sectPr>
        </w:pPr>
        <w:r>
            <w:t xml:space="preserve">Gujarat Technological University</w:t>
        </w:r>
        <w:r>
            <w:tab />
            <w:t xml:space="preserve">SAL College of Engineering</w:t>
        </w:r>
        <w:r>
            <w:tab />
            <w:t xml:space="preserve">Page </w:t>
            <w:fldSimple w:instr="PAGE \* roman \* mergeformat">
                <w:rPr>
                    <w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman"/>
                    <w:lang w:val="en-US" w:fangs="en-US"/>
                </w:rPr>
            </w:fldSimple>
        </w:r>
    </w:p>
</w:footer>
```
However, if you want the Roman page numbers to appear in the footer, you would need to use a section break and apply the Roman page numbering to the section that includes the "DECLARATION" to "TABLE OF CONTENTS" part.

To do this, you would add a section break before the "DECLARATION" section and another section break after the "TABLE OF CONTENTS" section.

Original XML:
```xml
<w:p><w:r><w:t>DECLARATION</w:t></w:r></w:p>
...
<w:p><w:r><w:t>TABLE OF CONTENTS</w:t></w:r></w:p>
```

New XML:
```xml
<w:p>
    <w:pPr>
        <w:sectPr>
            <w:pgNumType w:fmt='lowerRoman' w:start='1'/>
        </w:sectPr>
    </w:pPr>
    <w:r>
        <w:t>DECLARATION</w:t>
    </w:r>
</w:p>
...
<w:p>
    <w:pPr>
        <w:sectPr>
            <w:pgNumType w:fmt='lowerRoman' w:start='1'/>
        </w:sectPr>
    </w:pPr>
    <w:r>
        <w:t>TABLE OF CONTENTS</w:t>
    </w:r>
</w:p>
```
Then, you would remove the footer reference in the first section (if you don't want the page number to appear on the first page) and add the new footer with the Roman page number.

Original XML:
```xml
<w:p><w:r><w:t>Gujarat Technological University</w:t></w:r></w:p>
```

New XML:
```xml
<w:p>
    <w:pPr>
        <w:sectPr>
            <w:footerReference w:type="default"/>
            <w:pgNumType w:fmt='lowerRoman' w:start='1'/>
        </w:sectPr>
```

Describe me Ahmedabad in 10 points and tell me why ahmedabd is very popular among crowd ?

Here are 10 points that describe Ahmedabad:

1. **Location**: Ahmedabad is the largest city in the state of Gujarat, located in western India, on the banks of the Sabarmati River.
2. **Historical Significance**: Ahmedabad has a rich history dating back to 1411 AD, when it was founded by Sultan Ahmed Shah, and has been an important center of trade, commerce, and culture.
3. **Cultural Heritage**: The city is known for its rich cultural heritage, with a blend of Hindu, Muslim, and Jain influences, reflected in its architecture, cuisine, and festivals.
4. **Textile Industry**: Ahmedabad is often referred to as the "Manchester of India" due to its thriving textile industry, which has been a major contributor to the city's economy.
5. **Gujarati Cuisine**: The city is famous for its delicious Gujarati cuisine, which includes popular dishes like dhokla, khakhra, and Gujarati thali.
6. **Educational Hub**: Ahmedabad is home to many prestigious educational institutions, including the Indian Institute of Management (IIM), the National Institute of Design (NID), and the Gujarat Technological University (GTU).
7. **Economic Growth**: Ahmedabad has experienced rapid economic growth in recent years, with a strong focus on IT, pharmaceuticals, and manufacturing industries.
8. **Tourist Attractions**: The city has many popular tourist attractions, including the Sabarmati Ashram, the Akshardham Temple, and the Calico Museum of Textiles.
9. **Festivals and Celebrations**: Ahmedabad is known for its vibrant festivals and celebrations, including the Navratri festival, the Uttarayan (Kite Festival), and the Ahmedabad International Literature Festival.
10. **Infrastructure**: The city has a well-developed infrastructure, with a modern international airport, a comprehensive public transportation system, and a wide range of accommodation options.

As for why Ahmedabad is very popular among the crowd, here are a few reasons:

* **Business Opportunities**: Ahmedabad offers a favorable business environment, with a strong focus on entrepreneurship and innovation, making it an attractive destination for startups and entrepreneurs.
* **Cultural Richness**: The city's rich cultural heritage, reflected in its architecture, cuisine, and festivals, makes it a fascinating place to visit and explore.
* **Educational Opportunities**: Ahmedabad's reputation as an educational hub, with many prestigious institutions, attracts students and families from all over India.
* **Gujarati Hospitality**: The city is known for its warm and welcoming people, who are proud of their culture and traditions, making visitors feel at home.
* **Economic Growth**: Ahmedabad's rapid economic growth has created many job opportunities, making it an attractive destination for professionals and job seekers.
* **Food and Nightlife**: The city's vibrant food scene, with a wide range of delicious Gujarati cuisine options, and its growing nightlife, with many bars, clubs, and lounges, make it a popular destination for foodies and party-goers.

Overall, Ahmedabad's unique blend of history, culture, education, and economic growth makes it a popular destination among tourists, students, professionals, and entrepreneurs.
Now tell me what is famous food and festival in there ?

Famous Food in Ahmedabad:

1. **Dhokla**: A popular Gujarati snack made from fermented rice and split chickpeas, served with a spicy green chutney.
2. **Khakhra**: A thin, crispy flatbread made from wheat flour, often served with a variety of toppings like cheese, chutneys, and vegetables.
3. **Gujarati Thali**: A traditional meal consisting of a variety of dishes like dal, rice, roti, vegetables, and sweet dishes like shrikhand and jalebi.
4. **Khaman**: A steamed cake made from gram flour, served with a spicy green chutney and a dollop of butter.
5. **Jalebi**: A sweet, crispy fried dessert made from fermented batter, soaked in syrup and served hot.
6. **Undhiyu**: A traditional Gujarati curry made with a mixture of vegetables like eggplant, potatoes, and green beans, cooked in a spicy coconut-based sauce.
7. **Handvo**: A savory cake made from a mixture of lentils, rice, and vegetables, often served as a snack or breakfast item.
8. **Fafda**: A crispy, fried snack made from gram flour, often served with a side of chutney or a cup of tea.

Famous Festivals in Ahmedabad:

1. **Navratri**: A 9-day festival celebrated in honor of the divine feminine, with traditional garba and dandiya raas dances, music, and cultural events.
2. **Uttarayan (Kite Festival)**: A 2-day festival celebrated on January 14th and 15th, where people fly kites, enjoy traditional food, and participate in cultural events.
3. **Diwali**: The festival of lights, celebrated with fireworks, decorations, and traditional sweets and snacks.
4. **Holi**: The festival of colors, celebrated with colors, music, and traditional sweets and snacks.
5. **Janmashtami**: A festival celebrated to mark the birth of Lord Krishna, with traditional dances, music, and cultural events.
6. **Ganesh Chaturthi**: A festival celebrated to mark the birth of Lord Ganesha, with traditional dances, music, and cultural events.
7. **Ahmedabad International Literature Festival**: A literary festival that brings together authors, poets, and literary enthusiasts from around the world to celebrate literature and culture.

Other notable festivals and events in Ahmedabad include:

* **Sabarmati Riverfront Festival**: A cultural festival celebrated on the banks of the Sabarmati River, with music, dance, and cultural events.
* **Ahmedabad Heritage Walk**: A guided tour of the city's historical landmarks and cultural heritage sites.
* **Gujarat International Food Festival**: A festival that showcases the state's culinary diversity, with traditional food stalls, cooking competitions, and cultural events.
tell me about riverfront ?

The Sabarmati Riverfront in Ahmedabad is a 10.5 km long waterfront area along the Sabarmati River, which has been transformed into a vibrant public space. Here are some interesting facts about the Sabarmati Riverfront:

**History**: The Sabarmati River has been an integral part of Ahmedabad's history and culture, with the city being founded on its banks in 1411. However, over the years, the river had become polluted and neglected.

**Revitalization**: In the early 2000s, the Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation (AMC) launched a massive revitalization project to restore the riverfront to its former glory. The project aimed to improve the river's water quality, create public spaces, and promote sustainable development.

**Key Features**:

1. **Riverfront Promenade**: A 10.5 km long promenade along the river, perfect for walking, jogging, and cycling.
2. **Public Parks**: Several public parks and gardens, including the Sabarmati Riverfront Park, which offers stunning views of the river.
3. **Riverfront Plaza**: A large public plaza with cultural and entertainment facilities, including an amphitheater, a museum, and a convention center.
4. **Water Sports**: The riverfront offers various water sports, such as boating, kayaking, and paddle-boarding.
5. **Food and Beverage**: A range of food and beverage options, including restaurants, cafes, and street food stalls.
6. **Cultural Events**: The riverfront hosts various cultural events, including music concerts, theater performances, and festivals.
7. **Light and Sound Show**: A daily light and sound show, which showcases the city's history and culture.
8. **Public Art**: Several public art installations, including sculptures, murals, and interactive art pieces.
9. **Green Spaces**: The riverfront has several green spaces, including parks, gardens, and playgrounds.
10. **Community Engagement**: The riverfront offers various community engagement activities, including yoga classes, meditation sessions, and art workshops.

**Impact**:

1. **Economic Growth**: The Sabarmati Riverfront has contributed to Ahmedabad's economic growth by attracting tourists, businesses, and investments.
2. **Environmental Benefits**: The project has improved the river's water quality, reduced pollution, and created a green oasis in the heart of the city.
3. **Social Benefits**: The riverfront has become a popular public space, promoting social interaction, community engagement, and cultural exchange.
4. **Recreational Activities**: The riverfront offers a range of recreational activities, improving the overall quality of life for residents and visitors.

1. **Length**: The walkway stretches for 10.5 km, making it one of the longest riverfront walkways in India.
2. **Width**: The walkway is 10-12 meters wide, providing ample space for pedestrians, cyclists, and joggers to move around comfortably.
3. **Surface**: The walkway is made of high-quality, smooth, and durable material, making it suitable for walking, cycling, and jogging.

8. **Signage**: The walkway has clear signage, including directional signs, informational signs, and warning signs, which help users navigate the walkway and stay safe.

**Features along the Walkway**:

1. **Public Art**: The walkway features several public art installations, including sculptures, murals, and interactive art pieces.

**Activities and Events**:

Overall, the Sabarmati Riverfront Walkway is a world-class facility that has transformed the city's landscape and provided a safe, convenient, and enjoyable space for users to engage in various activities."

"'Original XML/Text' -> 'New XML/Text': HBK (H.B. Kapadia) School is a well-known educational institution in Gujarat, India, with multiple branches across the city of Ahmedabad and other parts of the state.

**About HBK School**:

HBK School was founded by the late Shri H.B. Kapadia, a renowned educationist and philanthropist, with the aim of providing quality education to students from all backgrounds. The school is known for its emphasis on academic excellence, extracurricular activities, and character development.

**Branches of HBK School**:

HBK School has multiple branches across Ahmedabad and other parts of Gujarat, including:

1. **HBK School, Ahmedabad**: The main branch of HBK School is located in Ahmedabad, with separate campuses for primary, secondary, and higher secondary sections.
2. **HBK School, Maninagar**: This branch is located in the Maninagar area of Ahmedabad and offers classes from primary to higher secondary.
3. **HBK School, Naranpura**: This branch is located in the Naranpura area of Ahmedabad and offers classes from primary to higher secondary.
4. **HBK School, Bapunagar**: This branch is located in the Bapunagar area of Ahmedabad and offers classes from primary to higher secondary.
5. **HBK School, Gandhinagar**: This branch is located in the Gandhinagar district of Gujarat and offers classes from primary to higher secondary.
6. **HBK School, Mehsana**: This branch is located in the Mehsana district of Gujarat and offers classes from primary to higher secondary.
7. **HBK School, Himmatnagar**: This branch is located in the Sabarkantha district of Gujarat and offers classes from primary to higher secondary.

**Curriculum and Facilities**:

HBK School follows the Gujarat State Education Board (GSEB) curriculum, with a focus on providing a well-rounded education that includes academics, sports, and extracurricular activities. The school has modern facilities, including:

1. **Air-conditioned classrooms**: All classrooms are air-conditioned, providing a comfortable learning environment for students.
2. **State-of-the-art laboratories**: The school has well-equipped laboratories for physics, chemistry, biology, and computer science.
3. **Library**: The school has a well-stocked library with a wide range of books, journals, and digital resources.
4. **Sports facilities**: The school has sports facilities, including a playground, basketball court, and indoor sports complex.
5. **Transportation**: The school provides transportation facilities for students, with a fleet of buses and vans.

**Extracurricular Activities**:

HBK School offers a range of extracurricular activities, including:

1. **Sports**: The school has teams for cricket, football, basketball, and other sports, with coaching and training provided by experienced professionals.
2. **Music and dance**: The school has music and dance classes, with opportunities for students to perform in school events and competitions.
3. **Art and craft**: The school has art and craft classes, with opportunities for students to showcase their work in school exhibitions and competitions.
4. **Debating and public speaking**: The school has a debating and public speaking club, with opportunities for students to participate in inter-school competitions.
5. **Community service**: The school has a community service club, with opportunities for students to participate in volunteer work and social service activities.

Overall, HBK School is a reputable educational institution in Gujarat, known for its academic excellence, extracurricular activities, and commitment to character development."

To achieve the desired changes, we need to modify the existing XML structure. Here's the updated XML:

Original XML:
```xml
<w:footer>
    <w:p>
        <w:r>
            <w:t>Gujarat Technological University</w:t>
        </w:r>
    </w:p>
</w:footer>
```

New XML:
```xml
<w:footer>
    <w:p>
        <w:pPr>
            <w:sectPr>
                <w:pgNumType w:fmt='lowerRoman' w:start='1'/>
            </w:sectPr>
        </w:pPr>
        <w:r>
            <w:t xml:space="preserve">Gujarat Technological University</w:t>
        </w:r>
        <w:r>
            <w:t xml:space="preserve">                                    </w:t>
        </w:r>
        <w:r>
            <w:t xml:space="preserve">SAL College of Engineering</w:t>
        </w:r>
        <w:r>
            <w:t xml:space="preserve">                                    </w:t>
        </w:r>
        <w:r>
            <w:t xml:space="preserve">Page </w:t>
        </w:r>
        <w:r>
            <w:fldSimple w:instr="PAGE \* Roman \* MERGEFORMAT">
                <w:rPr>
                    <w:noProof/>
                </w:rPr>
                <w:t>ii</w:t>
            </w:fldSimple>
        </w:r>
    </w:p>
</w:footer>
```

Changes Made:
- Added `<w:sectPr>` to the footer to include page numbering
- Added `w:pgNumType` with `w:fmt='lowerRoman'` to format page numbers as Roman numerals
- Added `w:start='1'` to start page numbering from 1
- Added Gujarat Technological University and SAL College of Engineering to the footer, separated by tabs
- Added page number field (`<w:fldSimple w:instr="PAGE \* Roman \* MERGEFORMAT">`) to display the current page number in Roman numerals

Note: These changes will apply to the entire document. If you want to apply these changes only to specific sections, you'll need to add section breaks and modify the footer settings for each section accordingly.

'"Original XML" -> "New XML"' 

However, in the context given here is 
'"<w:footer><w:p><w:r><w:t>Gujarat Technological University</w:t></w:r></w:p></w:footer>" 
-> 
"<w:footer><w:p><w:pPr><w:sectPr><w:pgNumType w:fmt='lowerRoman' w:start='1'/></w:sectPr></w:pPr><w:r><w:t xml:space="preserve">Gujarat Technological University</w:t></w:r><w:r><w:t xml:space="preserve">                                    </w:t></w:r><w:r><w:t xml:space="preserve">SAL College of Engineering</w:t></w:r><w:r><w:t xml:space="preserve">                                    </w:t></w:r><w:r><w:t xml:space="preserve">Page </w:t></w:r><w:r><w:fldSimple w:instr="PAGE \* Roman \* MERGEFORMAT"><w:rPr><w:noProof/></w:rPr><w:t>ii</w:t></w:fldSimple></w:r></w:p></w:footer>"'

1776331059.title: nothing is changed from the de...

messages.content: nothing is changed from the declartion to table of connatins in the footer add the romen page no and aslo add gujarat technology of univercity in the footer left side and sal college of enggeering in the right side

To accomplish the requested changes, we'll need to modify the OOXML code in the `document.xml` file. Specifically, we'll add a section break, modify the footer to include the page number in Roman numerals, and add the university and college names to the footer.

Original XML:
```xml
<w:footerReference />
```
New XML:
```xml
<w:pPr>
  <w:sectPr>
    <w:pgNumType w:fmt='lowerRoman' w:start='1'/>
    <w:footer>
      <w:p>
        <w:r>
          <w:t>Gujarat Technological University</w:t>
        </w:r>
        <w:tab />
        <w:r>
          <w:t xml:space='preserve'>Page </w:t>
        </w:r>
        <w:r>
          <w:fldSimple w:instr='PAGE \* Roman \* MERGEFORMAT'>
            <w:rPr>
              <w:noProof />
            </w:rPr>
            <w:t>i</w:t>
          </w:fldSimple>
        </w:r>
        <w:tab />
        <w:r>
          <w:t>SAL College of Engineering</w:t>
        </w:r>
      </w:p>
    </w:footer>
  </w:sectPr>
</w:pPr>
```
This modification will add a footer with the university name on the left, the Roman page number in the center, and the college name on the right, starting from page `i`. The `w:pgNumType` element is used to specify the page numbering format, and the `w:fldSimple` element is used to insert the page number. 

Please note that this is a general representation, and the exact XML code may vary depending on the specific requirements of your document and the version of the OOXML standard being used. 

Additionally, you may need to adjust the `w:start` attribute in the `w:pgNumType` element to set the correct starting page number for the Roman numeral page numbering. 

Also, to apply this modification to the specific section of the document (from declaration to table of contents), you would need to insert the section break (`<w:sectPr>`) at the beginning of that section. 

To achieve this, you can insert the following code at the start of the declaration section:
```xml
<w:pPr>
  <w:sectPr>
    <!-- footer definition here -->
  </w:sectPr>
</w:pPr>
```
Make sure to replace `<!-- footer definition here -->` with the actual footer code provided above. 

This will ensure that the modified footer is applied only to the specified section of the document. 

If you need to apply the same footer to multiple sections, you can insert the section break at the beginning of each section and specify the same footer definition.

To implement the changes you've requested, I'll outline the steps and provide a detailed explanation. 

### Changes to the Footer

You want to make the following changes to the footer:
1. **Add Roman Page Numbers**: Include Roman numerals (I, II, III, etc.) as page numbers in the footer.
2. **Add Gujarat Technological University (GTU) to the Footer Left Side**: This involves placing the university's name on the left side of the footer.
3. **Add SAL College of Engineering to the Footer Right Side**: Similarly, this involves placing the college's name on the right side of the footer.

### Implementation Steps

Here's how you can achieve these changes:

#### Step 1: Edit the Footer

*   **Access the Footer Section**: Depending on the document type (Word, PDF, LaTeX, etc.), you'll need to access the footer section. For example, in Microsoft Word, you can do this by double-clicking on the footer area at the bottom of the page.
*   **Remove Existing Content (If Any)**: Clear any existing content in the footer to start fresh.

#### Step 2: Add Roman Page Numbers

*   **Insert Page Numbers**: Use the "Insert Page Number" or "Page Number" feature available in your document editing software. Ensure you select the "Roman Numerals" option to display page numbers as I, II, III, etc.

#### Step 3: Add University and College Names

*   **Add Gujarat Technological University (GTU) to the Left Side**:
    *   Type out "Gujarat Technological University" or use an abbreviation if preferred (e.g., "GTU").
    *   Align this text to the left side of the footer.
*   **Add SAL College of Engineering to the Right Side**:
    *   Type out "SAL College of Engineering".
    *   Align this text to the right side of the footer.

#### Step 4: Formatting and Adjustment

*   **Adjust Font and Size**: Ensure the font and size of the text in the footer are consistent and readable.
*   **Spacing and Alignment**: Double-check the spacing between the page numbers and the names to ensure everything is aligned properly and aesthetically pleasing.

### Example of What the Footer Might Look Like

The footer, after applying the above changes, could look something like this:

**Left Side**                                **Center**                                 **Right Side**
Gujarat Technological University         I                                         SAL College of Engineering

Replace "I" with the actual Roman numeral page number that increments with each page.

### Conclusion

By following these steps, you should be able to update your document's footer to include Roman page numbers and the names of Gujarat Technological University and SAL College of Engineering, as per your requirements.

Primary background: #17172a
Sidebar background: #1e1e2e
Surface/card background: #1e1e34 to #252538
Borders: #3a3a4a to #4a4a6a (0.5px, subtle)
Accent/brand color: #5b4af7 (purple)
Secondary accent (text highlights, logo glow): #7b6ef8 to #8b7ef7
Primary text: #e0def8
Secondary text: #a8a6cc to #c8c6e8
Muted text: #6a6890 to #7a78a8
Active status green: #4caf6e

Typography:

Font: system sans-serif
Heading weight: 500
Body weight: 400
Sizes: 28px hero, 14px sidebar names, 12–13px body/buttons, 10–11px labels

Components:

Sidebar: 220px wide, dark #1e1e2e, items have 7px rounded rows, active row #2e2b52
Buttons/pills: border-radius: 22px, dark fill, 0.5px purple-tinted border
Input box: border-radius: 12px, dark fill #202035, subtle border
Logo icon: border-radius: 10px, solid #5b4af7 fill
AI avatar icon: 64px, border-radius: 18px, dark surface with purple diamond SVG
Top bar: minimal, action links in muted gray, Kali Pro pill with dark bg

Overall feel: Dark, soft purple-navy. Nothing pure black. Borders visible but whisper-thin. Text bright enough to read easily without being harsh white.

"Old color palette" -> "New dark purple UI theme with primary background #17172a, sidebar background #1e1e2e, and accent color #5b4af7"
"Light background" -> "#1e1e34 to #252538 surface and card background"
"Green accent" -> "#5b4af7 purple accent and #7b6ef8 to #8b7ef7 secondary accent"
"Black text" -> "#e0def8 primary text and #a8a6cc to #c8c6e8 secondary text"
"Bold font" -> "System sans-serif font with 500 heading weight and 400 body weight"
"Sharp corners" -> "7px rounded rows in sidebar, 22px border-radius for buttons and pills, and 12px border-radius for input boxes"
"Pure black borders" -> "0.5px subtle borders with #3a3a4a to #4a4a6a color"
"Harsh white text" -> "Bright but soft #e0def8 primary text and #a8a6cc to #c8c6e8 secondary text"
"Old logo" -> "New logo icon with border-radius: 10px and solid #5b4af7 fill"
"Old top bar" -> "New minimal top bar with action links in muted gray and Kali Pro pill with dark background"
